sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Let me check what changed after pressing LEFT. I need to compare the previous frame to the current frame to see which object moved.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Compare before and after to see what moved
before = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation
after = current_frame.segmentation

# Get node positions before and after by boundary
def get_node_map(seg):
    return {n['id']: n for n in seg['nodes']}

before_map = get_node_map(before)
after_map = get_node_map(after)

# Check which objects changed position
print("Objects that moved (boundary changed):")
for nid in after_map:
    if nid in before_map:
        if before_map[nid]['boundary'] != after_map[nid]['boundary']:
            print(f"  id={nid} color={after_map[nid]['color']}")
            print(f"    before: {before_map[nid]['boundary']}")
            print(f"    after:  {after_map[nid]['boundary']}")

# Also check if new nodes appeared or old ones disappeared
before_ids = set(before_map.keys())
after_ids = set(after_map.keys())
print(f"\nNew nodes: {after_ids - before_ids}")
print(f"Gone nodes: {before_ids - after_ids}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

- The dark frame (id=11) boundary changed slightly

- The bottom bar (id=13) changed slightly

print()

# Check what the new node 14 is

for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['id'] == 14:
        print(f"New node 14: color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']} boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
